title: Handpainted Lemon Pitcher
description:
We call it a brocca — a pitcher for the table. Our artisans in Vietri sul Mare throw each one on the wheel, shape the spout and handle by hand, and paint every detail freehand. The result is a generous, round-bellied jug that sits well in the hand and pours cleanly.

The decoration on this one is classic Amalfi Coast: plump yellow lemons with green leaves and curling vines, painted against a deep blue background. Wide yellow bands frame the top and bottom of the painted area, and the base and inner neck are left in clean white. The handle and rim are edged in blue. It's a colour combination you see all along the coast — blue and yellow, sea and lemons.

Available in several sizes, from a smaller table jug to a fuller pitcher. People use them for water, wine, or juice at dinner. Some customers fill them with wildflowers and keep them on a sideboard. Either way works.

Part of our Piazzetta Collection. Everything in this line shares the same lemon-and-blue palette, so you can pair the pitcher with matching plates, bowls, or cups if you like.
excerpt: A round-bellied ceramic pitcher painted with lemons on a deep blue ground. Thrown on the wheel in Vietri sul Mare.
seoTitle: Handpainted Lemon Pitcher — Ceramiche Da Mario
seoDescription: Handmade ceramic pitcher (brocca) with lemon motif, painted freehand by artisans in Vietri sul Mare. Piazzetta Collection by Ceramiche Da Mario.
